title: AI vs ML vs Deep Learning — Frontier Engineering
key_insight: The taxonomy is dissolving: foundation models trained on the internet are becoming the substrate everything else builds on, redefining what 'training' means.
body:
## The Paradigm Shift: Pre-training + Alignment

The modern AI stack has three tiers:

```
Tier 1: Foundation Model (pre-training on massive data)
  └── GPT-4, Llama-3, Gemini, Claude
      Training data: internet text, code, images
      Parameters: 7B–1T+
      Cost: $1M–$100M+

Tier 2: Fine-tuning + Alignment
  └── RLHF, DPO, instruction tuning
      Makes the model safe, helpful, focused
      Cost: $10K–$1M

Tier 3: Application Layer
  └── Prompting, RAG, tool use, function calling
      Where most product engineers work
      Cost: API calls ($0.001–$0.10 per 1K tokens)
```

## RLHF: Aligning Deep Learning with Human Values

```python
# Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (simplified)
# This is what makes ChatGPT useful instead of just fluent

# 1. Supervised fine-tuning on demonstration data
base_model.fine_tune(human_written_responses)

# 2. Train reward model from human preferences
# Human sees two responses, picks the better one
reward_model.train(comparison_pairs)  # learns what humans prefer

# 3. RL to maximise reward while staying close to base
from trl import PPOTrainer
trainer = PPOTrainer(model=base_model, reward_model=reward_model)
trainer.train()  # optimise model to produce high-reward outputs
```

## Emergent Capabilities: The Scale Surprise

Large language models exhibit capabilities not present in smaller models:

```
Parameters    Capabilities that emerge
─────────────────────────────────────────
< 1B          Basic completion, simple QA
7B            Multi-step reasoning begins
13B+          Code generation, complex logic
70B+          In-context few-shot learning
175B+         Chain-of-thought emerges spontaneously
```

These capabilities were not explicitly trained — they emerged from scale. This is deeply surprising and not fully understood.

## The Classical vs Deep Learning War: Outcome

Deep Learning has not replaced classical ML. It has expanded the pie:
- Tabular: classical ML (XGBoost) still dominates
- Vision/Text/Audio: deep learning dominates
- Hybrid: deep features + classical heads (common in production)
- Foundation model era: fine-tuning obsoletes many task-specific architectures
common_mistakes: Building custom task-specific models from scratch in 2024 — foundation model fine-tuning almost always wins
Neglecting alignment — a capable but unaligned model is dangerous and useless in production
Treating RLHF as magic — it optimises a reward model, which may not capture true human values
mini_exercise: Read OpenAI's original InstructGPT paper (2022). Identify the three stages: pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, and RLHF. What problem does each stage solve? What did they observe about model capability vs helpfulness?
